# BP-140 variant negative: sqlx StructScan returns an error to the caller.
lang: go
file: BP-140-variant-safe.go
variant: sqlx-structscan-checked
---
package sample

import (
	"fmt"
	"github.com/jmoiron/sqlx"
)

type User struct{}

func loadUser(db *sqlx.DB, user *User) error {
	rows, err := db.Queryx("SELECT id FROM users")
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("query users: %w", err)
	}
	if !rows.Next() {
		if closeErr := rows.Close(); closeErr != nil {
			return fmt.Errorf("close rows: %w", closeErr)
		}
		return nil
	}
	if err := rows.StructScan(user); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("scan user: %w", err)
	}
	if closeErr := rows.Close(); closeErr != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("close rows: %w", closeErr)
	}
	return nil
}
